title: Implement mailbox with selective receive
purpose: Message passing is how processes communicate — there is no shared state. The mailbox is a per-process queue where messages arrive from any sender and are consumed by selective receive (pattern matching against the queue, taking the first match, deferring the rest). Without this brief, processes are deaf. This brief delivers the two-part mailbox (MPSC arrival queue + scan list for selective receive), message copying into the receiver's heap, and the save-pointer mechanism that avoids rescanning already-tested messages.
task: Implement the Mailbox struct in crates/beamr/src/mailbox/. The mailbox has two parts: (1) an arrival queue backed by a lock-free MPSC channel (crossbeam-channel SegQueue or similar) where any process can deposit messages, and (2) a scan list (VecDeque<Term>) owned by the receiving process for selective receive. When the process begins a receive, pending messages are drained from the arrival queue into the scan list. The process scans the list with a save pointer — messages before the pointer have already been tested and failed to match. When a message matches, it is removed from the list and the save pointer resets. When no message matches and the list is exhausted, the process suspends (Waiting status). Implement message send as a deep copy of the term into the receiver's heap.

requirements.title: Mailbox struct with MPSC arrival queue
requirements.spec: beamr::mailbox SHALL define a Mailbox struct containing a lock-free MPSC arrival queue (the 'outer' queue that senders push to) and a VecDeque<Term> scan list (the 'inner' list that the owning process reads from). Mailbox::new() creates an empty mailbox. The arrival queue handle SHALL be cloneable so multiple senders can hold references.
acceptance: Mailbox::new() creates an empty mailbox with zero messages
Mailbox exposes a sender handle that implements Clone + Send
The scan list is private to the owning process

requirements.title: Message send with deep copy
requirements.spec: WHEN process A sends a message to process B THE SYSTEM SHALL deep-copy the term from A's heap into B's arrival queue. The copy SHALL be independent — modifying A's original SHALL NOT affect B's copy. For immediates (small int, atom, pid, nil), copying is trivial (they are values, not pointers). For boxed terms (tuples, lists, binaries), the copy SHALL recursively copy all heap-allocated data. The send operation SHALL NOT require the sender to hold a lock on the receiver's mailbox.
acceptance: Sending a small integer arrives as the same value in the receiver's mailbox
Sending a tuple deep-copies the tuple and its elements
Sending a list deep-copies all cons cells
The sender does not block or lock the receiver's scan list
Multiple senders can send concurrently to the same mailbox

requirements.title: Selective receive with save pointer
requirements.spec: beamr::mailbox::selective SHALL implement the BEAM selective receive algorithm. WHEN a process enters a receive expression THE SYSTEM SHALL: (1) drain pending messages from the arrival queue into the scan list, (2) scan the list starting from the save pointer, (3) for each message, test it against the provided match function, (4) if a message matches: remove it from the list, reset the save pointer to 0, return the message, (5) if no message matches: advance the save pointer to the end, return None (the process should suspend). The save pointer avoids rescanning messages that already failed to match in a previous scan.
acceptance: A receive with one matching message returns it and removes it from the list
A receive with no matching messages returns None
After a failed scan, the save pointer is at the end of the list
New messages arriving after a failed scan are tested from where the save pointer left off
After a successful match, the save pointer resets to 0
Messages before the save pointer are not re-tested

requirements.title: Mailbox message count and drain
requirements.spec: Mailbox SHALL provide message_count() returning the total number of messages (arrival queue + scan list). Mailbox SHALL provide drain_arrival() that moves all pending messages from the arrival queue into the scan list. drain_arrival is called at the start of each receive attempt.
acceptance: message_count() returns 0 for an empty mailbox
After sending 3 messages, message_count() returns 3
drain_arrival() moves messages from arrival queue to scan list
After drain, messages are accessible via selective receive

requirements.title: Unit and concurrency tests for mailbox
requirements.spec: WHEN the mailbox is tested THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests for: basic send/receive, selective receive with matching and non-matching messages, save pointer behaviour, concurrent sends from multiple threads, message ordering (FIFO), and deep copy verification (modifying sender's term after send does not affect receiver).
acceptance: cargo test -p beamr mailbox passes with all tests green

Tests cover: empty receive returns None, send then receive returns the message, selective receive skips non-matching, save pointer advances correctly, concurrent sends from 4 threads all arrive, message order is FIFO within a single sender

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement receive timeouts (after N milliseconds) — timer brief scope
SHALL NOT implement process suspension/resume — scheduler brief manages when a waiting process is re-run
SHALL NOT implement monitoring or exit signal delivery to mailbox — supervision brief scope
SHALL NOT implement binary-optimised message copying — deep copy all terms uniformly
SHALL NOT implement mailbox size limits or back-pressure — add later if needed
verification: cargo test -p beamr mailbox passes with all tests green
Code inspection: arrival queue is lock-free MPSC (crossbeam or equivalent)
Code inspection: send does not take a lock on the scan list
